title: Swartzia panamensis
intro: Swartzia panamensis, also known as Panaman tree or Panama tree, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, native to Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Swartzia panamensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Swartzia panamensis is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Swartzia, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the Brazilian pepper trees or guanacaste trees. The family Fabaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes legumes and other related species. Swartzia panamensis is a tropical plant native to Central and South America. It is classified as a tree species, with some sources indicating that it can grow up to 20 meters in height.
